title: NVIDIA — data center
tagline: Same brand-faithful Mode A direction applied to a product/solution landing page. Static photo hero replaces home's carousel; introduces flow-step and use-case 50/50 patterns; reuses home's chrome, geo-banner, and tile vocabulary.

direction.phrase: follow the current NVIDIA design and apply improvements that preserve the current design principles
movements: brand-faithful: palette + type inherited from approved home canon
hero: static single-slide (carousel pattern does not apply to product landings)
tile vocabulary: variant A (news + customer stories), variant G (architecture grid, text-led)
new patterns: flow-4-step (Cloud → Office → Data Center → Edge), use-case 50/50 (full-bleed image + text-block, alternating sides)

palette.name: NVIDIA brand-pinned
brandFaithfulInversions: saturated NVIDIA green #76b900 retained as single CTA accent

originalCritique: The live data-center page renders the same site-wide structural issues as home (no semantic landmarks, hidden H1 scaffold, mega-menu eager-rendered, fragmented CTA verbs). Layout-wise it ships an 8+ slide hero where one anchor would carry the whole story, and the architecture grid alternates between text-led and photo-led tiles within the same row, breaking the visual cadence. Workload tiles repeat marketing copy that the captured pages already say above; the news rail mid-page collides with the closing customer-stories rail because both use the same tile variant.

variants.name: Brand-faithful refresh
variants.thesis: Same chrome and brand surface as the approved home, applied to a product/solution landing template. Static single-slide hero, architecture grid in one consistent text-led variant, news and customer stories use distinct tile variants so the cadence stays clean. New flow-step and use-case 50/50 patterns introduced for content shapes home doesn't have.
variants.fix: Reuse the approved chrome — header, geo-banner, footer lift verbatim from home-proposed. Static hero — single full-bleed photo, eyebrow + H1 + lead + primary CTA, no auto-rotation. Consistent grid cadence — architecture row uses variant G (text-led, dense) for all four tiles; news rail and customer-stories rail use distinct tile variants. New patterns — flow-4-step (mono step numbers, hairline connectors) for the Cloud → Office → Data Center → Edge sequence; use-case 50/50 (full-bleed image + text-block, alternating sides) for the three workload features. Same site-wide gains as home: real landmarks, single visible H1, 8-verb CTA list, lazy mega-menu.
